description: The Genovese crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families that dominate organized crime in New York City and New Jersey. Founded by Charles 'Lucky' Luciano in the 1930s, it is the oldest and largest of the Five Families. Known for its secrecy and white-collar rackets, it has been considered the most powerful Mafia family in the United States. The family is currently led by Liborio 'Barney' Bellomo.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Genovese crime family ( Italian: [dʒenoˈveːze, -eːse] ), also sometimes referred to as the Westside , is an Italian American Mafia crime family
country_links.context: The Genovese crime family has ties to the Italian Mafia, as indicated by its Italian name and origins.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Genovese family is the oldest and the largest of New York's Five Families, and was the first of the New York families to expand into New Jersey.
country_links.context: The Genovese crime family operates across multiple states including New York, New Jersey, Florida, Connecticut, Massachusetts, and Pennsylvania.
